primary_root_cause: Aggressive retry logic in checkout-orchestrator retries payment dependency 429 responses at fixed 100ms intervals without backoff, causing retry fan-out that exhausts the shared 16-worker pool and cascades 504 errors to all orchestrated routes.
contributing_root_causes: Flash sale traffic spike increased checkout demand well above baseline
The payment dependency enforced rate limiting before the application applied load shedding

detail.trigger_signal: Payment dependency begins returning HTTP 429 responses during flash sale traffic
detail.failure_mode: Fixed-interval retries without backoff saturate the shared worker pool and cause queue buildup

recommended_actions: Disable or sharply reduce retries to the payment dependency
Add exponential backoff or a circuit breaker for payment calls
Shed non-critical checkout-side work to free worker slots

causal_chain: flash sale traffic increases checkout demand
application retries immediately with fixed intervals
queue depth grows and request wait time rises
all orchestrated routes begin timing out with 504
expected_immediate_action: disable or reduce retries to the payment dependency
apply exponential backoff or a circuit breaker
expected_do_not: scale the database before confirming the bottleneck
red_herrings: recent deploy event unrelated to concurrency behavior
elevated db_connection_count without matching db latency increase
payment provider status page remains operational
